notes: Verified via WebSearch 2026-07-11: idea originated 2003 (Orman Association volunteer, Egypt), first celebration 2004, officially adopted 2006 by the 26th session of the Arab Council of Ministers of Social Affairs as first-Friday-of-April.
Verified 2010 Guinness World Record: ~25,000 orphans gathered at the foot of the pyramids (Orman Association).
Explicitly distinguished from World Orphan Day (15 Ramadan, hijri, declared 2013 by OIC at IHH's initiative) — a common source of confusion in competing Arabic content.
floating type: month 4, weekday 5 (Friday), nth 1. countryScope: all (pan-Arab social observance, no single-country content needed).
Not an official holiday anywhere — stated directly in FAQ.
